creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors argues that small local models can handle most inference workloads more cost-effectively than frontier models, and provides a framework for deciding when to replace frontier calls with smaller models. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary and methods for measuring model performance on production traces to make data-driven model selection decisions.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together form a superior architecture compared to monolithic skills. He contends that this composition-based approach enables smaller models to perform real work, improves reliability and cost efficiency, and moves AI from internal tools to customer-facing products.

videos.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
videos.summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI has commoditized code generation, making requirements elicitation and stakeholder alignment the new bottleneck in software development. He presents three practical tools for this work: story mapping, a four-question value framework, and the VAD thinking path, using a VisualLabs hackathon case study where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to lack of business value, not technology limitations.

videos.title: Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
videos.summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses infrastructure design for autonomous AI agents that operate reliably in production environments. The talk addresses the gap between deterministic microservice infrastructure and non-deterministic LLM-based agents, covering orchestration, observability, retry handling, safety guardrails, and failure recovery patterns.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's agentic engineering whitepaper, arguing that the model accounts for only 10% of AI coding success while the surrounding harness of context, tools, and guardrails determines the other 90%. He discusses the spectrum from ad hoc coding to agentic systems, the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles, and why context engineering and harness engineering are the critical skills to invest in.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a cross-agent memory layer that stores context outside individual AI tools and allows different agents to retrieve shared encrypted memories. The service provides user-owned, encrypted memory management with granular access control across multiple AI providers.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The model is described as impressively performant relative to its size and cost.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use self-prompting loops instead of manual prompts, where an orchestrator agent spawns worker agents in parallel to complete tasks. This approach compounds costs and hallucinations but enables autonomous long-running operation without human intervention.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason covers a new project involving Crabbox and a codebase harness with associated skills for setup. The GitHub repo at AI-Builder-Club contains resources for developers, alongside references to Superdesign and the AI Builder Club learning platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer, a framework or tool for building AI agents. The content includes setup instructions, a GitHub template, and a workshop for implementing the approach in practice.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive AI agents that improve themselves through feedback loops. It covers foundational concepts of autonomous AI systems, use cases in ads optimization and SEO, and tools like memory and agent frameworks that enable self-improving behaviors.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and /Mission exploration capabilities for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers how to set up and prompt goals, comparing these features to prior autonomous coder systems and positioning them as the next generation of long-horizon task automation.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6 and its regulatory status as the AI industry matures. The video compares models from Anthropic and notes that GPT 5.6 faces similar scrutiny. OpenAI is consolidating its model lineup to three variants: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining how Generative Pretrained Transformers (GPT) work, covering core components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The tutorial walks through the basic mechanics underlying models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: This video explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram language model as an introduction to LLM mechanics. It covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, softmax, optimizers like AdamW, and backpropagation as building blocks toward understanding attention and GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 series in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants with a distinctive architecture combining hybrid Mamba transformer design, MTP, and Latent Mixture of Experts. The models represent an alternative approach to standard transformer architectures in open-source language modeling.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: A 7-step framework for system design: Requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach prioritizes understanding the problem before selecting technology, using a URL shortener as a case study example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling enables AI agents to request and execute external functions rather than rely solely on language model outputs. The technique involves the model requesting a tool, a framework executing it, and looping to process results, transforming agents from guessing systems into data-aware decision makers.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and guardrails for AWS service integration. It improves agent success rates by replacing outdated or incomplete general knowledge with tested procedures and current documentation.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock to enable secure on-premises deployment without data leaving AWS infrastructure. The tutorial covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation at scale. The video compares code generation practices across OpenAI Codex, Claude Code, and Google's internal tools, with speculation on adoption trends through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech produces a video discussing Malus, a company or tool positioned as potentially ending or disrupting open source development. The video covers how Malus operates, its impact on open source maintainers, and examines whether the company's practices are harmful to the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: Why We're Not Too Late to Stop AI Hackers | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke argues that AI has dramatically lowered the barrier to launching cyberattacks, enabling two people with a GPU to execute what once required elite teams and years of preparation. He advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture and identifies three behavioral signals that reveal attackers already inside networks.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, and operational scale rather than code alone. He contends that AI's threat to SaaS is overstated and that the best AI products succeed by creating raving fans through strong product experience.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that upcoming breakthroughs in robotics will be driven by design for human interaction and delight rather than intelligence or utility alone. She critiques the humanoid-focused approach of companies like 1X and Unitree as myopic, predicting billions of diverse robots will shape the future.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses why large companies like Google use spreadsheets for strategic planning and how Pigment built an enterprise AI performance management platform to consolidate finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She shares insights on competing against legacy software vendors, the importance of hiring decisions, and the role of trust in enterprise sales growth.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Slash AI Costs: Smart Model Choice for Max ROAI! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof advises matching model complexity to task requirements rather than defaulting to high-capability models. The strategy aims to maximize return on AI investment through cost-effective model selection.

videos.title: AI Ecosystems: Claude vs. SAP/Salesforce & Living Artifacts Explained! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses trade-offs in AI ecosystems, comparing expensive AI queries against self-updating living artifacts. The video examines cost-effectiveness considerations for integrating AI into dynamic systems like those provided by SAP and Salesforce.

videos.title: Unlock Free SaaS with Claude Artifacts: Your AI Power-Up! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof presents Claude Artifacts as free SaaS functionality for sales, research, and productivity tasks. The video highlights how to use Claude's artifact feature as a cost-free alternative to paid software tools.

videos.title: AI Prices Soar: Fable's Fall & China's Rise? #shorts
videos.summary: Frontier AI models are becoming too expensive and vulnerable. Chinese open-source alternatives are emerging as cheaper options, potentially reshaping the global AI landscape amid rising geopolitical tensions.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
